# Pre Submission Document

Group 26

Domain 10

Team members: Balachandra Vignesh Shunmugam, Rajesh Kumar, Sivasankari Ramasubramanian

# 1. Business Problem Understanding and Objective:

About the company: BuildMax Infrastructure does large construction projects and is facing frequent schedule delays, budget overruns and occurrence of safety incidents. Currently there is no proactive monitoring. Late projects and budget overspends cut into profits, lead to disputes and legal trouble, and damage the company’s reputation.

The Business problem: Currently, BuildMax has no reliable way to identify risks related to delay in schedule or budget overrun or likely occurrence of safety incidents well ahead. Current data projects problems after they have occurred. We have to do a predictive analysis

# 2. Challenges and Constraints

| **Challenge / Constraint**  | **What it means and how we handle it**                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|-----------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Class imbalance**         | Only about 22% of projects in the data are delayed, need to handle this imbalance                                                                                                                                                                              |
| **Data quality issues**     | The data has duplicate rows, the same category written in different ways (one project type is spelled three ways), about 10% missing values in two columns, and at least one impossible cost value. We catch these while profiling the data and clean them up. |
| **Non-informative columns** | Some columns are just ID numbers or old system codes with no useful pattern, and one cost column simply repeats another. The project phase column also has no link to whether a project is delayed. We drop these and explain why.                             |
| **Undefined objective**     | The brief does not state the exact goal; we have to define it ourselves. So we clearly write down the targets we chose, the columns we left out, and the assumptions we made.                                                                                  |

# 2. Background Study / Literature Review

Cost overrun risk assessment\[3\] paper highlights the different models and its comparison with the training and evaluation models used. Multiple papers suggest neural networks \[1\]\[5\]. Different pre-processing steps and feature engineering with different models are detailed in paper\[6\]. The references section contains the list of literature reviews attached. Different models used in research papers attached commonly include Random Forest, ANN, Logistic Regression, SVM and Ensemble\[11\].

# 3. Solution Approach

Our key business objectives include prediction of project delay, cost overrun and safety incident occurrence. We need to identify key drivers behind predicted risk for decision support.

| Problem Statement | Model(s) | Tolerance to Bias | Tolerance to variance | Validation technique | Evaluation metrics |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Predict Project Delay (Binary classification) | RF, NB, XGB, NN with softmax | Low - should avoid false negatives ie missing delayed projects | Medium - high. Variance acceptable if early warnings are provided for high-risk projects | K-fold cross validation | F1, recall, ROC-AUC, cross entropy |
| Cost prediction | SVR, XGB, linear regression, ANN, Ensemble | Low to medium - as it involves cost predictions, needs to be more accurate | Low - finance forecasting | K-fold cross validation | R2, RMSE |
| Safety incident occurrence | RF, XGB, Ensemble, NN with softmax | Low - as it involves safety | Medium - false alarms can be acceptable | K-fold cross validation | Recall, F1, Cross-entropy |

# 4. Workflow Diagram

<img alt="Model Workflow" src="data:image/png;base64," style="width:100%; max-width:900px;" />

Fig 1.1 Model Workflow

The workflow transforms historical data into early-warning risk predictions with driver-level explanations, allowing project, procurement, finance, and safety teams to intervene before delays/cost escalations become severe.

# 5. Proposed Timeline

| Activities                                        | Timeline        |
|---------------------------------------------------|-----------------|
| Pre-submission document                           | 28th June, 2026 |
| Domain analysis and data visualization            | 12th July, 2026 |
| Feature engineering and Pre-processing completion | 12th July, 2026 |
| Machine learning modeling                         | 16th Aug, 2026  |
| Metrics comparison and hyper parameter tuning     | 16th Aug, 2026  |
| Business recommendations and predictive analysis  | 29th Aug, 2026  |
| Final presentation and documentation              | 6th Sep, 2026   |

# 6. Expected Deliverables / Outputs

As an outcome of our project, we will produce the following deliverables

1.  Technical documentation including the following

    1.  Data visualization and analysis

    2.  Machine learning model(s) used

    3.  Metrics comparison between the different machine learning models

    4.  Dashboard for project-level and portfolio-level risk review

    5.  Business recommendations and mitigation

2.  Github repository link(s)

3.  Presentation with the summary of approach, results and recommendations

# 7. References

1.  [Machine Learning Application in Construction Delay and Cost Overrun Risks Assessment](https://www.mdpi.com/2075-5309/16/3/651)

2.  [Machine learning applications for predicting safety incidents in construction industry](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-025-34763-0)

3.  [A Comparative Assessment of Concrete Compressive Strength Prediction at Industry Scale Using Embedding-Based Neural Networks, Transformers, and Traditional Machine Learning Approaches](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.09096)

4.  [Cost Overrun Risk Assessment and Prediction in Construction Projects: A Bayesian Network Classifier Approach](https://www.mdpi.com/2075-5309/12/10/1660?utm_campaign=releaseissue_buildings&utm_medium=email&utm_source=releaseissue&utm_term=titlelink236)

5.  [Leveraging AI for Predicting Delay Risks in Construction Project Management: Emerging Trends and Research Gaps](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Jostina-Dhimitri-2/publication/398797855_An_Analytic_Study_of_Stakeholders'_Perceptions_on_environmental_Responsibility_and_Natural-Based_Tourism_in_Adriatic_Coastal_Zone_of_Albania_pg_31-42/links/694301697e61d05b5310206e/An-Analytic-Study-of-Stakeholders-Perceptions-on-environmental-Responsibility-and-Natural-Based-Tourism-in-Adriatic-Coastal-Zone-of-Albania-pg-31-42.pdf#page=98)

6.  [Predicting the duration of goods transportation delays based on machine learning methods](https://www.emerald.com/mscra/article-pdf/7/3/404/10312782/mscra-02-2025-0011en.pdf)

7.  [Predicting the Compressive Strength of High Strength Concrete Using Machine Learning Algorithms: A Comparative Analysis of Regression Models -](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Mohammed-Shaaban-8/publication/386320897_Predicting_the_Compressive_Strength_of_High_Strength_Concrete_using_Machine_Learning_Algorithms_A_Comparative_Analysis_of_Regression_Models/links/67d28b59d759700065087fdd/Predicting-the-Compressive-Strength-of-High-Strength-Concrete-using-Machine-Learning-Algorithms-A-Comparative-Analysis-of-Regression-Models.pdf)

8.  [Deep Multilayer Perceptron Neural Network for the Prediction of Iranian Dam Project Delay Risks](https://ascelibrary.org/doi/abs/10.1061/JCEMD4.COENG-12367)

9.  [Real-time prediction of project duration deviation using CNN-LSTM-FIS](https://www.emerald.com/bepam/article-abstract/16/2/304/1331885/Real-time-prediction-of-project-duration-deviation?redirectedFrom=fulltext)

10. [Predicting the Indirect Cost of Construction Projects in Egypt: An Artificial Neural Network Approach](https://www.proquest.com/openview/2050a9c5cc757f58e73af775a1ed8aa1/1?pq-origsite=gscholar&cbl=2026366&diss=y)

11. [Ensemble learning framework for forecasting construction costs](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0926580524006393)

12. [Predicting cost overrun in construction projects using machine learning algorithms: the case of Jordan](https://www.emerald.com/ecam/article-abstract/33/6/4732/1259970/Predicting-cost-overrun-in-construction-projects?redirectedFrom=fulltext)
